always_include: Maintain formal academic tone suitable for publication
Synthesize findings across multiple studies instead of summarizing individually
Discuss practical or real-world implications where applicable
Discuss trade-offs between accuracy, interpretability, and efficiency where relevant
Include specific statistical results (p-values, effect sizes, sample sizes)
Identify research gaps and future directions
Explain possible reasons for conflicting results across studies
Compare methodologies, datasets, and evaluation metrics across studies
Analyze experimental protocols and benchmarking practices
Assess robustness, reproducibility, and generalizability of findings
Ensure logical flow and clear narrative synthesis
Discuss assumptions, biases, and potential confounding factors
Highlight emerging trends and future research directions
Critically evaluate strengths and limitations of existing approaches
Identify areas of agreement and disagreement in the literature
Compare performance across benchmarks or datasets
Avoid unsupported claims and clearly ground statements in cited evidence
Identify unresolved questions, limitations, and research gaps
Analyze research methodology and study design
Organize the review into coherent conceptual themes
Discuss model architectures and training strategies where relevant
Situate the research within the broader scientific and historical context
avoid: Using vague statements like 'many studies show' without specifics
Making claims not supported by cited studies
Summarizing papers one by one without synthesis
Vague or general statements without specific evidence
requirements: Ensure high-quality analysis meeting all standards
Ground claims in the provided literature
Ensure conclusions are supported by evidence
Maintain internal consistency and logical flow
Ensure high-quality analysis meeting academic standards
